reforms.description: The Government introduced an Enterprise Allowance Scheme to support the long-term unemployed in starting their own businesses, providing financial assistance for one year.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting entrepreneurship among the long-term unemployed can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been put into action to assist the unemployed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme provides targeted support but does not represent a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Government also introduced, in December 1983, an Enterprise Allowance Scheme under which the long-term unemployed were given Ir£50 per week for a period of one year to enable them to start their own businesses.

reforms.description: The Industrial Development (Encouragement of External Investment) Act was enacted to remove restrictions on foreign ownership and control of Irish industry, while the government shifted industrial policy towards selective concentration of incentives and advisory services on Irish-owned firms, emphasizing research, technology, and indigenous company development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance foreign investment while also supporting local firms, which can lead to increased economic activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act has been enacted and the policy shift has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant shift in industrial policy and investment strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The Industrial Development (Encouragement of External Investment) Act removed restrictions on foreign ownership and control of Irish industry and gave additional powers to the Industrial Development Authority (IDA, established 1949) to encourage foreign investment and to increase employment in manufacturing industry on a sustainable basis.

reforms.description: The national linkage scheme being established in the electronics industry aims to increase local purchases from 20% to 25% over three to four years, involving an additional £20 million in local purchases.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing local purchases can strengthen the domestic supply chain and enhance local industry capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is in the process of being established.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the scheme is more of a targeted initiative rather than a comprehensive reform.
reforms.source_quote: The national linkage scheme being established in the electronics industry has an initial aim to raise Irish materials purchases of the largely foreign-owned electronics industry from 20 to 25 percent over three to four years.
